task.prompt:
Open the GitHub PR URL from the input row. On the PR page, extract the author's GitHub
  username from the header. Then:                                                        
  1. Use `search` to query Google for: "<author name> site:linkedin.com/in"     
  2. Use `goto_search_result` with index=0 and url_filter="linkedin.com/in" to open the
  first LinkedIn profile result.                                                         
  3. Take a full-page screenshot of the LinkedIn profile into folder `<repo_slug>/` where
   <repo_slug> is the repo path with slashes replaced by dashes.                         
  4. Extract the profile fields from the visible content — set any field to null if it's 
  not visible.                                                                           
                                                                                         
  For any click whose target depends on page content, emit `visual_do` with a            
  natural-language intent. Do NOT guess selectors.

samples.verdict_reason: LinkedIn profile fields (school, current_company, tenure) are all null because the evidence shows only a LinkedIn authwall screenshot, meaning profile content was never accessed.
samples.error: Error: Page.title: Execution context was destroyed, most likely because of a navigation

samples.verdict_reason: Required fields 'school' and 'tenure' are null, and the author field contains a display name rather than the GitHub username specified by the task.
samples.error: Error: Page.title: Execution context was destroyed, most likely because of a navigation

samples.verdict_reason: The only evidence is a LinkedIn authwall screenshot, which cannot support extraction of school or current_company, and tenure is null; also the screenshot wasn't saved into the required repo-slug folder.

samples.verdict_reason: All required non-null fields are populated with plausible values and a LinkedIn profile screenshot artifact exists; tenure is null which is acceptable per instructions.
